world.headline: Artemis II Splashes Down After Historic Moon Mission
world.body: NASA's Artemis II mission successfully returned astronauts to the Moon for the first time in over 50 years, with the crew safely splashing down in the Pacific Ocean. While hailing the mission as a triumph, NASA acknowledged more work is needed to make future Artemis flights a reality. The agency said it will continue to refine the spacecraft and systems to ensure the long-term sustainability of lunar exploration.

sources.title: NASA hails success of Artemis moon mission but says more work to do
Artemis II marks historic lunar return with Pacific splashdown success
Title: Artemis II astronauts journey back to Earth after Moon mission
Moment Artemis II splashes down after moon mission
Artemis II mission was a triumph - now comes the hard part

world.headline: Djibouti's President Wins Sixth Term Amid Opposition Concerns
world.body: Djibouti's President Ismail Omar Guelleh has won a sixth consecutive term in office, securing 97.8% of the vote. The election was criticized by opposition groups, who alleged irregularities and a lack of a level playing field. Guelleh, who has ruled the strategic Horn of Africa nation since 1999, dismissed the concerns, saying the vote was 'free and transparent'.

sources.title: Djibouti's president wins unprecedented sixth term with 97.8% of vote
Djibouti’s President Guelleh wins sixth straight term in office

world.headline: Tensions Flare in Gaza as Israel Rejects Ceasefire with Hezbollah
world.body: At least seven Palestinians have been killed in recent Israeli airstrikes on Gaza, as tensions escalate in the region. Israel has rejected a ceasefire proposal with Hezbollah, ahead of talks next week with Lebanon. The attacks come amid concerns over the potential for a wider conflict, with the US and Iran also set to hold talks in the coming days that could have significant implications for the region.

sources.title: At least seven Palestinians killed in Israeli attacks on Gaza
Israel rejects ceasefire with Hezbollah ahead of Lebanon talks next week
Israeli drone attack kills Palestinians near Gaza mosque
Iran war: What is happening on day 43 of the US-Iran conflict?

world.headline: UK Delays Chagos Islands Deal Amid US Opposition
world.body: The UK has decided to hold off on a deal to cede the Chagos Islands to Mauritius, amid opposition from the United States. The Chagos Islands, which were part of the British Indian Ocean Territory, have been a source of longstanding dispute, with Mauritius claiming sovereignty over the archipelago. The UK's decision to delay the deal comes as it navigates the complex geopolitical landscape surrounding the strategically important islands.

sources.title: UK to hold off on deal ceding Chagos Islands amid US opposition

ai.headline: France Ditches Windows for Linux, Citing US Tech Risks
ai.body: The French government is moving away from Windows and towards Linux, citing concerns over the strategic risks posed by US technology. This decision comes as part of a broader effort to reduce reliance on American tech giants and strengthen France's digital sovereignty. The shift is seen as a significant step towards greater technological independence and a potential model for other European nations looking to reduce their exposure to US-based platforms and services.

sources.title: France's government is ditching Windows for Linux, says US tech a strategic risk

ai.headline: Artemis II Splashes Down After Daring Moon Mission
ai.body: NASA's Artemis II mission has successfully concluded, with the four astronauts safely returning to Earth after a daring journey around the Moon. The mission marks a significant milestone in the Artemis program, paving the way for future crewed missions to the lunar surface. As the Artemis program moves forward, NASA must now determine its next steps, balancing the program's ambitious goals with budgetary and technical challenges.

sources.title: The Artemis II mission has ended. Where does NASA go from here?
Four astronauts are back home after a daring ride around the Moon
Artemis II safely splashes down

ai.headline: AI Tool Recording Doctor Visits Sparks Californian Lawsuit
ai.body: Californians have filed a lawsuit over an AI tool that records doctor visits without patients' consent. The tool, which is used by some healthcare providers, has raised concerns about patient privacy and the potential misuse of sensitive medical data. The case highlights the growing tensions between the adoption of AI technologies in healthcare and the need to protect individual rights and personal information.

sources.title: Californians sue over AI tool that records doctor visits

ai.headline: Anthropic Bans OpenClaw Creator from Accessing AI Model
ai.body: Anthropic, the AI research company, has temporarily banned the creator of the OpenClaw tool from accessing its AI model, Claude. The move comes after concerns were raised about the reliability and stability of OpenClaw's interactions with the model. The incident underscores the challenges faced by AI developers in balancing the need for open access and the responsibility to ensure the safe and responsible use of their technology.

sources.title: Anthropic temporarily banned OpenClaw’s creator from accessing Claude
OpenClaw’s memory is unreliable, and you don’t know when it will break

btc.headline: Bhutan Dumps 70% of Bitcoin Holdings in 18 Months
btc.body: The tiny Himalayan nation of Bhutan has sold off a significant portion of its Bitcoin holdings, offloading 70% of its BTC in just 18 months. The country, which had previously embraced Bitcoin mining, may have also halted those operations entirely. This dramatic shift in Bhutan's crypto strategy comes as the country grapples with economic challenges and a need to shore up its finances.

sources.title: Bhutan has sold 70% of its bitcoin in 18 months. It may have stopped BTC mining too.

food.name: Moroccan Lamb Tagine
food.description: Slow-cooked lamb in a fragrant sauce of spices, dried fruits, and vegetables, served over couscous.
food.tip: Let the tagine simmer for at least 2 hours to allow the flavors to meld and the meat to become tender.

exercise.name: Yoga Flow for Flexibility
exercise.description: A dynamic yoga sequence focused on improving overall flexibility, with a emphasis on deep stretches for the hips, hamstrings, and back.

learn.description: An ancient Greek analog computer from the 2nd century BC, used to predict astronomical positions and eclipses.
learn.why: This remarkably sophisticated device, discovered in a shipwreck, is a testament to the advanced engineering and scientific knowledge of the ancient Greeks, and offers insights into the history of technology.
